# Issuance Foundation Checklist: Tax Document Issuance Foundation

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, completeness, and consistency of
requirements for the common tax document issuance foundation before task
generation.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based
on feature context and requirements. It tests requirements writing quality, not
implementation behavior.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all required common domain concepts defined with purpose, ownership, and target English names? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Spec Key Entities, Data Model]
- [x] CHK002 Are all five required document types specified with canonical names and external SRI code mappings? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Spec FR-003, Spec SRI Document Code Mapping]
- [x] CHK003 Are all required value objects and issuance concepts represented in the data model with fields and validation rules? [Completeness, Spec Key Entities, Data Model]
- [x] CHK004 Are all eleven application output ports documented with responsibilities and forbidden leakage rules? [Completeness, Spec FR-012, Contract application-port-contracts]
- [x] CHK005 Are durable documentation updates for canonical terminology and legacy-to-target mappings explicitly required? [Completeness, Spec FR-018, Plan Durable Documentation, Contract migration-mapping-update]
- [x] CHK006 Are out-of-scope runtime artifacts fully enumerated so task generation cannot infer REST, persistence, SRI, queue, webhook, or bootstrap work? [Completeness, Spec Scope Boundaries, Spec AR-005, Spec AR-006, Contract source-boundary]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is `AccessKey` validation defined with objective structural criteria rather than a vague validity statement? [Clarity, Spec FR-004, Data Model AccessKey]
- [x] CHK008 Are `DocumentState` and `AuthorizationState` clearly distinguished by purpose and lifecycle responsibility? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Data Model DocumentState, Data Model AuthorizationState]
- [x] CHK009 Are allowed state transitions specified with source state, target state, and trigger or rule? [Clarity, Spec State and Transition Rules, Data Model DocumentState]
- [x] CHK010 Is authorized-document immutability stated with clear exception handling requirements for future specifications? [Clarity, Spec FR-009, Spec State and Transition Rules]
- [x] CHK011 Is retry eligibility described as constrained candidate behavior where unresolved details remain PFV-bound? [Clarity, Spec FR-010, Spec PFV-ISS-004, Plan Pending Functional Validations]
- [x] CHK012 Is the local voiding rule clear about which states are rejected and which states require future validation? [Clarity, Spec FR-011, Spec State and Transition Rules, Data Model DocumentState]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 Are source-boundary requirements consistent between the spec, plan, and source-boundary contract? [Consistency, Spec AR-004 to AR-006, Plan Source Code, Contract source-boundary]
- [x] CHK014 Are application port names and responsibilities consistent between the spec, plan, data model, and port contract? [Consistency, Spec Application Ports, Plan Layer and Boundary Design, Data Model Application Ports, Contract application-port-contracts]
- [x] CHK015 Are idempotency keys and scopes consistent between functional requirements, plan, and future use case contract? [Consistency, Spec FR-015, Spec Idempotency Requirements, Plan Idempotency Rules, Contract future-use-case]
- [x] CHK016 Are audit event names consistent across spec, plan, and future use case contract without introducing adapter delivery behavior? [Consistency, Spec FR-016, Plan Audit Events, Contract future-use-case]
- [x] CHK017 Are SRI document codes consistently classified as external contract values and not internal target names? [Consistency, Spec FR-003, Spec SRI Document Code Mapping, Plan Naming and Migration Classification]
- [x] CHK018 Are Pending Functional Validations consistent between spec, plan, research, and migration mapping contract? [Consistency, Spec Pending Functional Validations, Plan Pending Functional Validations, Research Decisions, Contract migration-mapping-update]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Are success criteria measurable with counts or explicit pass/fail thresholds for concepts, states, ports, artifacts, and exclusions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK020 Does each success criterion trace to at least one functional, architectural, naming, or contract requirement? [Traceability, Spec FR-001 to FR-019, Spec AR-001 to AR-009, Spec NR-001 to NR-007]
- [x] CHK021 Are acceptance scenarios written as requirements-review scenarios rather than implementation test cases? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec User Scenarios and Testing]
- [x] CHK022 Are task-generation readiness criteria defined for source boundaries, durable documentation updates, and PFV exclusions? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan Traceability and Source of Truth, Contract source-boundary, Contract migration-mapping-update]
- [x] CHK023 Can reviewers objectively determine whether the feature remains within domain/application scope before tasks are generated? [Measurability, Spec AR-004 to AR-006, Plan Constitution Check]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK024 Are primary scenarios covered for common issuance language, lifecycle rules, application boundaries, and migration traceability? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1 to User Story 4]
- [x] CHK025 Are alternate scenarios covered for newly discovered legacy terms and future controlled exceptions to authorized immutability? [Coverage, Spec Pending Naming Decisions, Spec FR-009, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK026 Are exception scenarios covered for invalid transitions, duplicate issuance identity, retry ineligibility, and local voiding constraints? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec State and Transition Rules]
- [x] CHK027 Are future-feature handoff scenarios defined for invoice, credit note, debit note, withholding, waybill, retry, synchronization, and webhook use cases? [Coverage, Spec FR-019, Contract future-use-case]
- [x] CHK028 Are documentation handoff scenarios covered so later features consume durable mappings rather than temporary feature-only tables? [Coverage, Spec FR-018, Contract migration-mapping-update]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK029 Are edge cases documented for SRI codes being misused as internal names? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-003]
- [x] CHK030 Are edge cases documented for adapter DTOs, persistence entities, or SRI XML/SOAP models leaking inward? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-014, Contract application-port-contracts]
- [x] CHK031 Are edge cases documented for terminal states and delayed or conflicting SRI authorization outcomes? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Spec State and Transition Rules]
- [x] CHK032 Are edge cases documented for future features attempting to introduce excluded runtime layers too early? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases, Contract source-boundary]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK033 Are testability requirements specified for domain and application behavior without infrastructure or Quarkus runtime? [Non-Functional, Spec FR-013, Plan Testing, Contract source-boundary]
- [x] CHK034 Are audit sensitive-data exclusions explicit enough to cover secrets, credentials, tokens, signing material, certificates, and sensitive configuration? [Security, Spec Sensitive Data Exclusions, Plan Audit Events]
- [x] CHK035 Are reliability and repeatability requirements expressed through idempotency scope for issuance, retries, synchronization, webhook delivery, XML generation, and sequence assignment? [Reliability, Spec Idempotency Requirements, Plan Idempotency Rules]
- [x] CHK036 Are observability requirements for this foundation limited to canonical audit events without implying adapter logging implementation? [Observability, Spec FR-016, Plan Audit Events]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK037 Are assumptions about `docs/as-is/` versus future `docs/legacy/` location documented without conflicting with constitution-required durable locations? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions, Spec FR-018, Constitution Principle X: Specification-Governed Migration]
- [x] CHK038 Are dependencies on feature `001-canonical-terminology-boundaries` and canonical terminology governance explicitly documented? [Dependency, Spec Assumptions, Spec AR-009, Plan Traceability]
- [x] CHK039 Are deferred behaviors excluded from affected tasks unless resolved, excluded, or deferred with documented handling? [Dependency, Spec FR-017, Plan Pending Functional Validations, Contract future-use-case]
- [x] CHK040 Are build and runtime stack constraints documented without turning this foundation into framework-dependent domain code? [Constraint, Plan Technical Context, Contract source-boundary]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK041 Are there any remaining target-relevant Spanish legacy terms without a decided mapping or Pending Naming Decision path? [Ambiguity, Spec Migration Classification, Contract migration-mapping-update]
- [x] CHK042 Are there any contradictions between terminal state rules and future controlled-operation exceptions? [Conflict, Spec State and Transition Rules, Data Model DocumentState]
- [x] CHK043 Are there any conflicts between modeling `IssuanceMode` now and deferring runtime sync/async behavior to future specs? [Conflict, Spec IssuanceMode, Spec PFV-ISS-001, Research PFV-ISS-001 Decision]
- [x] CHK044 Are unresolved retry and signed-XML requirements clearly prevented from becoming implementation tasks in this feature? [Ambiguity, Spec PFV-ISS-004, Plan Pending Functional Validations, Contract future-use-case]

## Notes

- Focus areas: domain/application foundation, Clean Architecture source
  boundaries, application ports, SRI isolation, state mappings, PFV governance,
  idempotency, auditability, and durable migration documentation.
- Depth: Standard reviewer checklist before task generation.
- Audience/timing: Feature author and peer reviewer before `$speckit-tasks`.
